origin_explanation: from Gk. phouka, transcription of Heb. pūk 'black paint'
the translation is doubtful. Tayyä may have been misled by the occurrence of the word manbar 'chair', in Henoch 18:8.

phonetics: pilās, pilās TAN i TAN, (L) pilāsä TAN, (T.M) pilāso TAN
meaning: 'temple, pavilion, gate, (T.M) tent, (T) place for sacrificing'
origin_explanation: from Gk. pylēs, gen.sg of pylē 'gate'.

meaning: 'shield and spear, (M) soldier who carries a spear'
origin_explanation: from Gk. peltastēs 'carrier of a light shield'.
from Gk. phanē 'torch'; also in Tna.Amh. fana.
from Gk. pneuma 'spirit'; also päwnumä, pänəwämä, päwəwmä (Euringer, ZS 7[1929],74; Dobberahn 152).
from Gk. plinthion 'sundial'.

meaning: 'the difference in days between a solar year and a lunar year'
origin_explanation: is a misreading of t`ntayon T`T`T`T`, for which see below.

phonetics: papirā, paperā, päperā, pepirā T`T`G` i T`T`G` i T`T`G`, (K) papir T`T`G`
origin_explanation: from Gk. porphyra 'purple dye'; also Ar. firfir (Rüžička 6). See also papperā, below.
from Gk. porphyra 'purple dye'; also Ar. firfir (Rüžička 6). See also papperā, below.
from Gk. porphyra 'purple dye'; also Ar. firfir (Rüžička 6). See also papperā, below.
from Gk. papyros 'papyrus'
from Gk. peperi 'pepper'. See also barbarre, above.
see also perkā.
from Gk. presbyteros 'priest'. See also parəzporos.
from Gk. paskha 'Passover', transcription of Aram. pashā. See also päsikä, fäsikä, fäsh I
from Gk. pissa 'pitch'.
from Gk. paskha 'Passover', transcription of Aram. pashā. See also päsä.
is probably Tayyä's interpretation of päsikä I.

phonetics: paskimā, in paskimā həbəst T`H`H`, T`H`H` (Lt)
origin_explanation: see also pak`simā.
from Gk. psaltēs 'harper, psalmist, one who sings psalms in the church'. See also 'absalates.
